name: Onion Bhaji
recipeInstructions:
1. These appetizers are easy to make and can be shallow or deep fried. Find chick pea flour (besan) at an Indian grocery store.
2. Make sure the vegetable oil is heated to 350°F before adding food. A small bread cube browns in about 15 seconds, or you can test with a deep fry thermometer. Too cool oil causes everything to become limp and greasy. Drain on a rack or paper towel before serving. A wok makes an excellent deep fryer.
3. Cut the onions into halves and slice them to be about 1/4 inch thick. Beat together egg, flour, and enough water to make a batter as thick as whipping cream. Add spices and let rest for 15 minutes.
4. Stir the onions into the mixture and let sit for 5 minutes.
5. Heat about 1 inch of oil in a skillet over high heat until very hot. Drop small mounds of onion rings into the oil. Press down slightly with a spatula. Fry on one side until crisp and brown, then turn over and fry the second side. Remove bhajis as they cook. Keep warm in an oven set to 200°F.
6. Serve piled on a platter with mango chutney. SERVES: 3-4
